name: Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop
name_original: Puente de la Malena desde Peñacaballera – running circular por la Vía de la Plata
description: Jog the moderate 6.3-mile Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop, featuring varied terrain and the historic Roman Bridge of La Malena.
region_intro: Jogging around Peñacaballera offers a diverse landscape for runners. The region is characterized by its hilly terrain, with routes featuring notable elevation changes and views of natural features like reservoirs and historical paths. Runners can expect a mix of forest sections, open areas, and trails that traverse varied ground. The area provides opportunities for both moderate and more challenging running experiences. Best jogging routes around Peñacaballera The most popular jogging route is Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 12 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a section of the historic Vía de la Plata, offering varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is La Muela and La Cruz de Jeromo Loop, a difficult 5.2 miles (8.4 km) path. It features significant elevation changes and panoramic views from its high points. Local runners also love the View of Embalse de Baños – Hervás Old Town loop from Hervás, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through varied landscapes including views of Embalse de Baños, often completed in about 1 hour 12 minutes. Jogging around Peñacaballera is defined by its hilly landscapes, historical paths, and views of local reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate runs to more difficult trails. The routes in Peñacaballera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Peñacaballera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for running trails in Peñacaballera?
A: The running routes around Peñacaballera offer a mix of challenges. You'll find 16 moderate trails and 31 difficult trails, reflecting the region's hilly terrain and varied landscapes. There are no easy routes listed, so runners should be prepared for some elevation changes.

Q: What kind of distances can I expect on the running routes around Peñacaballera?
A: The trails in Peñacaballera vary in length, generally ranging from approximately 7.9 km (4.9 miles) to 11.1 km (6.9 miles). For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38173171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños Reservoir – Hervás Old Town loop from Hervás&lt;/a&gt; is about 7.9 km, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Embalse de Baños – Hervás Old Town loop from Hervás&lt;/a&gt; covers 11.1 km.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Peñacaballera?
A: While the guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, some trails might be suitable for active families, especially those with experience in hilly terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993696841/via-de-la-plata-penacaballera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.2 km route that follows a historic path, offering varied terrain that could be enjoyable for a family run.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails around Peñacaballera?
A: Many trails in rural areas like Peñacaballera are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. It's always a good idea to check local signage for specific restrictions, particularly in nature reserves or agricultural areas. The varied terrain and forest sections found on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CASTAÑAR GALLEGO HERVAS loop from Hervás&lt;/a&gt; could be great for a run with your canine companion.

Q: What can I see along the running routes in Peñacaballera?
A: The routes offer diverse scenery, including views of local reservoirs and historical paths. You can encounter natural attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5701178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chorrera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/904737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Cruz and Balanced Boulder&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy reservoir views like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5135086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails also feature viewpoints, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ambroz Valley and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in Peñacaballera?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Peñacaballera are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993696841/via-de-la-plata-penacaballera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991224462/la-muela-and-la-cruz-de-jeromo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Muela and La Cruz de Jeromo Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Peñacaballera?
A: The running experience in Peñacaballera is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, the presence of historical paths like the Vía de la Plata, and the scenic views of local reservoirs and natural features.

Q: Is public transport available to access the running trails in Peñacaballera?
A: Access to specific trailheads by public transport might be limited given the rural nature of Peñacaballera. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to nearby towns like Hervás, which serves as a starting point for several routes, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Embalse de Baños – Hervás Old Town loop from Hervás&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the running routes?
A: Absolutely. Peñacaballera's hilly terrain provides numerous opportunities for scenic views. You can find viewpoints offering panoramas of the Ambroz Valley and various reservoirs, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ambroz Valley and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/656367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Montemayor and the Baños Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991224462/la-muela-and-la-cruz-de-jeromo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Muela and La Cruz de Jeromo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is particularly noted for its panoramic views from high points.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Peñacaballera area?
A: There are over 45 running routes available for exploration around Peñacaballera, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Peñacaballera?
A: Given the region's hilly landscape and potential for varied weather, spring and autumn are generally ideal for running in Peñacaballera. The temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be hot, especially at midday, while winter might bring cooler temperatures and potentially more challenging conditions on some trails.
tips: Located in a wonderful and picturesque landscape in the interior of Salamanca, this small town is very quiet but at the same time offers many places to visit near here such as the Peña del Cuervo and the renowned Peña Negra within the Sierra de Candelario Natural Area.
Rebuilt in the 18th century, it is also part of the Mozarabic way to Santiago.
On reaching the town of Peñacaballera, this section of the Vía de la Plata runs through a beautiful, very green, wooded and picturesque countryside.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop?
faqs.answer: This jogging route is approximately 10.2 kilometers (6.3 miles) long. For an average jogger, it takes about 1 hour and 12 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop is classified as a moderate run. It features a positive elevation gain of about 300 meters and requires good fitness. Due to varied terrain, including natural and unpaved sections, sure-footedness is needed in some parts.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Peñacaballera Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop offers a diverse mix of surfaces. You'll encounter gravel paths, natural paths, and unpaved tracks, along with some paved sections and a small amount of asphalt. This variety provides a constantly changing underfoot experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Peñacaballera. You can typically find parking in or near the village square, which is a good starting point for the loop.

faqs.question: Is the Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop suitable for jogging with dogs?
faqs.answer: While the trail offers a natural setting, specific rules regarding dogs are not provided. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to carry waste bags.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The inland Salamanca region can experience varied weather. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. While winter jogging is possible, be prepared for potentially colder conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any notable sights or landmarks along the Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features several points of interest. You'll pass through the tranquil Peñacaballera Village Square and Fountain, and cross the historic Roman Bridge of La Malena over the Cuerpo de Hombre River. You'll also experience the scenic Peñacaballera Countryside, with views of the Sierra de Candelario Natural Area.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog the Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop. It is freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does this jogging route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Mtb Vía de la Plata, the Vía de la Plata hiking - 02 Merida to Salamanca, and the Cañada Real La Vizana, each covering about 37% of the loop.

faqs.question: What makes the Vía de la Plata - Peñacaballera Loop unique compared to other local jogging routes?
faqs.answer: This loop offers a concentrated experience of the ancient Vía de la Plata's character within a manageable distance. It uniquely combines the historical essence of the Roman route with the natural beauty of the Sierra de Candelario region, providing a dynamic jogging experience through varied terrain and picturesque landscapes.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Peñacaballera is a tranquil village. While specific facilities are not detailed, you may find local establishments in the village square for refreshments or restrooms before or after your run.

waypoints.name: Roman Bridge of La Malena over the Cuerpo de Hombre River
